notes: SIUBEN is the Dominican Republic's Sistema Único de Beneficiarios, a private government social-registry API with no public/machine-readable spec (web research found only the institutional site and open-data GIS hub, no developer docs). This OpenAPI 3.0.3 document is therefore synthesized from the adaptor surface. The adaptor is a thin HTTP wrapper: ast.json exposes no adaptor-specific operations, only re-exported language-common helpers plus an `http` namespace (http.get, http.request). The one CONFIRMED endpoint, read from src/Utils.js, is POST /authenticate — it takes {username, password} JSON and the adaptor stores response.body.Token as the bearer token for all subsequent calls. All other operations (listBeneficiarios, getBeneficiario, getHogar, getHogarMiembros, getIcv) are SYNTHESIZED/domain-plausible endpoints that the generic bearer-authenticated http.get would target, modeled from SIUBEN's documented public functions (cédula/beneficiary lookup, household 'hogar' records, and the Índice de Calidad de Vida 0–100 quality-of-life score). Paths, field names (Spanish) and shapes are best-effort inventions, not verified against the live API — suitable for mocking only. servers[0].url is a placeholder; the real base URL is provided per-connection via configuration.baseUrl. Common/utility exports (fn, each, field, cursor, etc.) are excluded as they make no external calls.
